name: Socket Security API
what_it_does: Analyzes open-source npm, PyPI, and other package dependencies for supply chain risks including malware, typosquatting, protestware, and suspicious behavior using deep package inspection.
use_cases: Scan a package.json or requirements.txt before installation to detect supply chain threats in an automated agent workflow
Query package risk scores and alerts to gate pull requests that introduce new or updated dependencies
Retrieve detailed package behavior reports (network access, filesystem writes, shell exec) to assess third-party risk
Monitor a set of packages over time and receive alerts when a previously safe package is compromised or updated suspiciously
Generate SBOM-enriched risk summaries for an application's full dependency tree to include in security reports
not_for: Runtime application security monitoring — Socket analyzes packages statically, not running processes
CVE-only vulnerability scanning already covered by tools like Snyk or Dependabot
Binary or container image scanning outside of supported package ecosystems
best_when: Best when an agent needs to evaluate the trustworthiness of open-source packages before they enter a codebase, particularly for detecting novel supply chain attacks that CVE databases miss.
avoid_when: Avoid when the only concern is known CVEs in dependencies — a dedicated CVE scanner will have more comprehensive vuln data for that use case.

auth.notes: API key passed as a Bearer token; keys are issued per organization. GitHub App integration available as an alternative auth path for repo-level access.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free tier available for public repositories and limited API lookups; Socket for GitHub is free for open-source projects
pricing.paid_tiers: Team plan starting around $10/month per contributor; Enterprise plans with SLA and private repo support available on request
pricing.notes: API access beyond the free tier requires a paid plan; public package lookups may be available without auth at reduced rate.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits vary by plan; free tier is throttled; exact limits not publicly documented

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Errors generally return meaningful HTTP status codes with short descriptions, but documentation on error codes is sparse.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Package scoring and report endpoints are read-only and fully idempotent; safe to retry without side effects.
known_agent_gotchas: Package scores can change between calls as Socket's analysis pipeline updates asynchronously; cache results carefully if consistency matters
The API distinguishes between 'alerts' (actionable findings) and 'scores' (aggregate risk), and agents often need both in separate calls
Ecosystem parameter is required for most package endpoints and must match exactly (e.g., 'npm', 'pypi') — mismatches return empty rather than errors
Bulk package analysis is not available via a single API call; agents must loop over packages individually, which can be slow for large lockfiles
Webhook payloads for new alerts require the receiving endpoint to handle deduplication, as the same package can trigger multiple alerts

security_score_components.security_notes: Ironically strong internal security practices for a security product; API keys are the only auth method with no granular scoping, which is a minor concern for multi-team setups.
